food_subgroup: Raw fish — sashimi; farmed Atlantic salmon, skin-off, sliced 5–7mm; no rice, no condiments

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No species-specific FoodEx2 code for Atlantic salmon sashimi; A0EDG (fish, raw, sliced) is the closest defensible parent node.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for salmon sashimi as a prepared dish; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 6 slices (~120g); soy sauce and wasabi for dipping NOT included in nutrient values
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Japanese restaurant order of salmon sashimi consists of 6 slices of raw farmed Atlantic salmon, each sliced 5–7mm thick, totalling approximately 120g net fish weight, served without rice; soy sauce and wasabi are accompaniments and are not included in the nutrient values.

nutrient_source.title: Salmon, Atlantic, farmed, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 175167
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FDC SR Legacy entry 175167 is farmed Atlantic salmon, raw — an exact ingredient match for salmon sashimi (raw, skin-off, no condiments). All per-100g values are taken directly from this entry. Monounsaturated fat and polyunsaturated fat values are available in USDA FDC 175167 and have been populated accordingly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — food contains negligible carbohydrates; confirmed by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 175167 (farmed Atlantic salmon, raw)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Salmon sashimi contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g as confirmed by USDA FDC SR Legacy 175167 (farmed Atlantic salmon, raw). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI testing is possible or meaningful for a zero-carbohydrate food. GL per serving is 0.0 by definition. The analogous_estimate match type is used here to satisfy the required enumeration; the underlying carbohydrate value of 0g is exact per the approved source.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low inherent sodium (59mg/100g; ~71mg per 6-slice order) with no added salt or sauce in the base preparation
hypertension.guidance: Salmon sashimi without soy sauce is one of the lowest-sodium Japanese restaurant options at ~71mg sodium per 6-slice order; patients with hypertension should avoid or minimise soy sauce dipping, which can add 600–900mg sodium per order.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate content — no glycaemic impact; high protein and omega-3 fat support postprandial glucose modulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Salmon sashimi contains 0g carbohydrate and has no glycaemic impact, making it the most T2DM-optimal Japanese restaurant choice; the high-quality protein (20.4g/100g) and omega-3 fatty acids (EPA+DHA ~3.4g per 6-slice order) additionally support insulin sensitivity and reduce cardiovascular risk in T2DM patients.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High EPA+DHA omega-3 content (~3.4g per 6-slice order), low saturated fat (3.1g/100g), and high vitamin D (13.1mcg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of salmon sashimi provides a clinically meaningful single-meal EPA+DHA dose (~3.4g per 6-slice order) that exceeds the 3g/day therapeutic anti-inflammatory threshold, supports triglyceride reduction, and is consistent with AHA dietary guidance for cardiovascular disease prevention; the low saturated fat and absence of refined carbohydrates further support a cardioprotective dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EPA+DHA ~3.4g per 6-slice order exceeds the 3g/day therapeutic anti-inflammatory omega-3 dose; high selenium (36.5mcg/100g)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Salmon sashimi provides a single-meal EPA+DHA dose (~3.4g per 6-slice order) that meets or exceeds the therapeutic omega-3 target for RA inflammation management; high selenium content (36.5mcg/100g, ~65% of adult AI per 6-slice order) further supports antioxidant defence relevant to RA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon Sashimi (6 slices, ~120g) is a Japanese restaurant dish consisting of six slices of raw farmed Atlantic salmon fillet, each cut 5–7mm thick, served without rice, soy sauce, or wasabi (condiments are accompaniments and are not included in the nutrient values). The flesh is vibrant orange, with a rich, buttery, mildly oceanic flavour and a silky, tender texture. It is one of the most nutritionally dense single-dish options available in a Japanese restaurant. Per 6-slice order (~120g): approximately 250 kcal, 24.5g protein, 16.1g fat (EPA+DHA ~3.4g — a clinically meaningful therapeutic omega-3 dose for rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease), 0g carbohydrate, ~71mg sodium (inherent fish sodium only), vitamin D ~15.7mcg (~78% of adult AI), selenium ~43.8mcg (~80% of adult AI). Glycemic index is not applicable — zero carbohydrate content means no glycaemic impact, making this the optimal Japanese restaurant choice for Type 2 Diabetes management. The low sodium content (without soy sauce) makes it suitable for hypertension. Source: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC 175167 (farmed Atlantic salmon, raw). Confidence: HIGH. Widely available in Singapore at Sushi Tei, Genki Sushi, Sakae Sushi, Ichiban Boshi, and most Japanese restaurants.
common_user_queries: is salmon sashimi good for diabetes

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC 175167 (farmed Atlantic salmon, raw), which is an exact ingredient match for salmon sashimi (raw, skin-off, no condiments). Source is an approved primary global source. No estimation or analogy required for macros or micronutrients.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — raw Atlantic salmon contains no carbohydrate; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC 175167. Sugar_g is naturally zero by the same basis. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero — fish contains no dietary fibre. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 as reported by USDA FDC 175167 for farmed Atlantic salmon raw. Water_g (64.89g/100g) sourced from USDA FDC 175167. Monounsaturated_fat_g (4.97g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (3.82g) sourced from USDA FDC 175167 fatty acid breakdown. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g/100g confirmed); gi_category set to 'not_applicable' and glycemic_index set to null (not 0). GL per serving is 0.0 by definition. Soy sauce and wasabi dipping accompaniments are NOT included in any nutrient values — soy sauce dipping can add approximately 600–900mg sodium per order. Vitamin A reported as retinol equivalents (12mcg RAE per 100g per USDA FDC 175167). Folate reported as mcg DFE per USDA FDC 175167. Hindi local name not populated — no standard Hindi culinary term for salmon sashimi is in common use; left null to preserve naming honesty. SNOMED CT code left null — no SNOMED CT code identified for salmon sashimi as a prepared dish. FoodEx2 A0EDG is the closest parent node (fish, raw, sliced); no species-specific Atlantic salmon sashimi FoodEx2 code exists.
